 {"id":"caam-j14","title":"Implement keyboard navigation and actions","description":"## Task
Wire up all keyboard shortcuts to their actions.

## Keybindings
- q: Quit
- ↑/↓: Navigate profiles
- Tab: Switch provider
- Enter: Run CLI with selected profile
- l: Login to selected profile
- o: Open account page in browser (requires browser epic)
- e: Edit profile
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter mode

## Implementation
- Handle key events in Update()
- Dispatch to appropriate actions
- Show confirmation dialogs for destructive actions

## Dependencies
- Requires browser epic for 'o' command
- Edit mode needs separate view state

## Acceptance Criteria

- Delete requires confirmation
- Actions provide visual feedback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.395351148-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all keyboard navigation and actions: fixed 'l' key conflict, added Edit (e), Search/filter mode (/), updated help view and detail panel keybindings display","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.409454362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-ta7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.410828349-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-nmm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.411813644-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"caam-j6rg","title":"Implement Claude credentials identity parser","description":"# Task: Implement Claude credentials identity parser

## Overview
Extract identity information from Claude Code credential files (.credentials.json) without API calls.

## Background
Claude stores identity differently from Codex. The .credentials.json file contains a claudeAiOauth object with:
- accountId
- subscriptionType: "max", "pro", etc.
- May contain email in some versions

## Technical Details

### Function
```go
// ExtractFromClaudeCredentials reads Claude .credentials.json and extracts identity
func ExtractFromClaudeCredentials(path string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read .credentials.json
    // 2. Parse JSON structure
    // 3. Extract claudeAiOauth object
    // 4. Map to Identity struct
}
```

### Claude Credentials Structure (observed)
```json
{
  "claudeAiOauth": {
    "accessToken": "...",
    "refreshToken": "...",
    "expiresAt": 1234567890,
    "accountId": "abc123",
    "subscriptionType": "max"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly parses Claude credentials format
- [ ] Extracts accountId and subscriptionType
- [ ] Handles missing fields gracefully
- [ ] Works with different Claude credential versions
- [ ] Sub-millisecond extraction time

## Test Cases
1. Parse valid credentials with all fields
2. Parse credentials with minimal fields
3. Handle corrupted JSON file
4. Handle missing file

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/identity/claude.go
- internal/identity/claude_test.go

-{"id":"caam-j7pp","title":"Implement caam cost command","description":"# Task: Implement caam cost command

## Overview
Create the caam cost command that displays token cost analysis, subscription value comparison, and usage trends.

## Command Interface
```
$ caam cost [provider] [flags]

Flags:
  --last duration    Time period to analyze (e.g., "7d", "30d", "24h")
  --format string    Output format: table (default), json, csv
  --profile string   Specific profile to analyze (default: all)
```

## Output Example
```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    TOKEN COST ANALYSIS (Last 30 Days)                  ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  SUMMARY                                                                ║
║  Total tokens:     8,234,567                                           ║
║    Input:          2,456,789 (30%)                                     ║
║    Output:         4,567,890 (55%)                                     ║
║    Cache read:     987,654 (12%)                                       ║
║    Cache create:   222,234 (3%)                                        ║
║                                                                         ║
║  MODEL BREAKDOWN                                                        ║
║  claude-3-opus     ████████████████░░░░ 78%   6,423,000 tokens  $189.23║
║  claude-3-sonnet   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 19%   1,564,567 tokens   $23.47║
║                                                                         ║
║  COST COMPARISON                                                        ║
║  If pay-per-token:  $213.01                                            ║
║  Your subscription: $200.00/month                                       ║
║  Savings this month: $13.01 (6%)                                       ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Technical Details

### Command Implementation
```go
var costCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "cost [provider]",
    Short: "Analyze token costs and subscription value",
    RunE: runCost,
}

func runCost(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // 1. Parse time period flag
    // 2. Scan logs for the period
    // 3. Aggregate by model
    // 4. Calculate costs using pricing tables
    // 5. Compare to subscription cost
    // 6. Render output
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows token breakdown by type (input/output/cache)
- [ ] Shows model breakdown with percentages
- [ ] Calculates accurate API-equivalent costs
- [ ] Compares to subscription cost when known
- [ ] Supports table, JSON, and CSV output
- [ ] Handles empty log directories gracefully

## Files to Create
- cmd/caam/cmd/cost.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/cost_test.go

 {"id":"caam-jdrl","title":"Implement token aggregation and daily rollup","description":"# Task: Implement token aggregation and daily rollup

## Overview
Create functions to aggregate LogEntry data into daily/weekly/monthly summaries with model breakdowns.

## Technical Details

### Aggregation Functions
```go
// Aggregate aggregates log entries into a TokenUsage summary
func Aggregate(entries []*LogEntry) *TokenUsage

// AggregateByDay groups entries by date and aggregates each day
func AggregateByDay(entries []*LogEntry) []*DailyUsage

// AggregateByModel groups entries by model
func AggregateByModel(entries []*LogEntry) map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
```

### DailyUsage Structure
```go
type DailyUsage struct {
    Date     string       // "2025-01-10"
    Usage    *TokenUsage
    ByModel  map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
}
```

 {"id":"caam-je9o","title":"E2E: Monitor and precheck commands with all output formats","description":"# Task: E2E Tests for Monitor and Precheck Commands

## Overview
End-to-end tests for the caam monitor and caam precheck commands with all output formats.

## Test Categories

### 1. Monitor Command Tests

#### Table Format
```go
func TestMonitorTableFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format table --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "table", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output length: %d bytes", len(output))
    t.Logf("[TEST] First 500 chars:\n%s", output[:min(500, len(output))])
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "LIVE USAGE MONITOR")
    require.Contains(t, output, "CLAUDE")
}
```

#### Brief Format (tmux integration)
```go
func TestMonitorBriefFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format brief --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "brief", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output: %q", output)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Length: %d chars", len(output))
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Less(t, len(output), 80, "Brief format should fit in status bar")
    require.Regexp(t, `\w+:\d+%`, output) // e.g., "claude:42%"
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestMonitorJSONFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format json --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "json", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] JSON output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    err = json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Parse result: err=%v", err)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "updated_at")
    require.Contains(t, result, "profiles")
}
```

### 2. Precheck Command Tests

#### Full Precheck Output
```go
func TestPrecheckFull(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "SESSION PLANNER")
    require.Contains(t, output, "RECOMMENDED")
}
```

#### Brief Format
```go
func TestPrecheckBrief(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format brief")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "brief")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Brief output: %q", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    // Should be a one-liner recommendation
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestPrecheckJSON(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format json")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "json")
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Keys: %v", keys(result))
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "recommended")
    require.Contains(t, result, "backups")
    require.Contains(t, result, "forecast")
}
```

### 3. Error Handling Tests
- No profiles configured
- Provider not supported
- Network errors (mock)
- Partial failures (some profiles error)

### 4. Integration Tests
- Monitor + jq piping
- Precheck + shell scripting
- Output to file

## Test Infrastructure

### Mock Server for Rate Limits
```go
func setupMockRateLimitServer(t *testing.T) *httptest.Server {
    return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        t.Logf("[TEST] Mock request: %s %s", r.Method, r.URL.Path)
        // Return mock rate limit data
    }))
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log exact command being run
- Log full stdout/stderr
- Log exit code
- Log any parsing errors
- Log timing of operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All output formats work correctly
- [ ] Monitor --once exits cleanly
- [ ] Precheck shows all sections
- [ ] JSON output is valid and parseable
- [ ] Brief output fits in 80 chars
- [ ] Errors are handled gracefully
- [ ] Tests work with mock data

- cmd/caam/cmd/precheck_e2e_test.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/testutil_test.go

 {"id":"caam-jig","title":"Headless Authentication Support (Device Code Flow)","description":"# Headless Server Support

## Status: Core Features Complete!

The primary headless workflows are now fully functional:

### ✅ Completed

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Device auth setup | Works via native \`codex login --device-auth\` + \`caam backup\` |
| Hit-limit → switch → resume | Works via \`caam activate\` + native \`codex resume\` |
| Vault transfer | ✅ \`caam export/import\` implemented (caam-zha) |
| Documentation | ✅ AGENTS.md updated (caam-1r6) |
| AuthModeDeviceCode constant | ✅ Added (caam-hzu) |

### Remaining (P2/P3 - Profile Isolation Mode Only)

These are for the **advanced** Profile Isolation mode, not the primary workflow:

| ID | Task | Priority | Purpose |
|----|------|----------|---------|
| caam-v89 | --device-code CLI flag | P2 | For \`caam login\` in Profile Isolation mode |
| caam-ela | Codex provider support | P2 | Implementation for above |
| caam-0gy | Session resume | P3 | For Profile Isolation mode only |

## The Working Workflow

```bash
# SETUP (on headless server)
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam

# HIT LIMIT → SWITCH → RESUME
caam activate codex account2             # < 1 second!
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Native Codex
```

## Closed Tasks

| ID | Reason |
|----|--------|
| caam-1xv | Research complete - Claude lacks good headless option |
| caam-ai6 | Research complete - vault transfer is best for Gemini |
| caam-e79 | Removed - headless detection was over-engineering |
| caam-m5a | Removed - QR code was gold-plating |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:00.94446079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","close_reason":"Core headless workflows complete: device auth setup, hit-limit→switch→resume, vault transfer, and documentation.  {"id":"caam-npy","title":"Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm

## Context
The core logic that determines which token is "fresher" and should be synchronized. This is the brain of the sync system.

## Token Freshness

### What Makes a Token Fresh?
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. A "fresher" token:
1. Has a later expiry time (primary criterion)
2. Was created/modified more recently (tiebreaker)

### Token Structure Analysis

#### Claude Token (`.claude.json`)
```json
{
  "oauthToken": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauthToken.expiry`

#### Codex Token (`auth.json`)
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734623340
}
```
Key field: `expires_at` (Unix timestamp)

#### Gemini Token (`settings.json`)
```json
{
  "oauth_credentials": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauth_credentials.expiry`

### Freshness Extractor
```go
package sync

// TokenFreshness represents the freshness of a token
type TokenFreshness struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    ModifiedAt  time.Time
    IsExpired   bool
    Source      string // "local" or machine name
}

// ExtractFreshness parses auth files to determine token freshness
func ExtractFreshness(provider, profile string, authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)

// CompareFreshness returns true if a is fresher than b
func CompareFreshness(a, b *TokenFreshness) bool {
    // Primary: later expiry wins
    if !a.ExpiresAt.Equal(b.ExpiresAt) {
        return a.ExpiresAt.After(b.ExpiresAt)
    }
    // Tiebreaker: later modification wins
    return a.ModifiedAt.After(b.ModifiedAt)
}
```

### Provider-Specific Extractors
```go
type FreshnessExtractor interface {
    Extract(authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)
}

func GetExtractor(provider string) FreshnessExtractor {
    switch provider {
    case "claude":
        return &ClaudeFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "codex":
        return &CodexFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "gemini":
        return &GeminiFreshnessExtractor{}
    }
}
```

## Sync Algorithm

### Sync Operation
```go
type SyncOperation struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    Direction   string    // "push" or "pull"
    Machine     *Machine
    LocalFresh  *TokenFreshness
    RemoteFresh *TokenFreshness
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Operation   *SyncOperation
    Success     bool
    BytesSent   int64
    BytesRecv   int64
    Duration    time.Duration
    Error       error
}
```

### Full Sync Algorithm
```go
func (s *Syncer) SyncWithMachine(m *Machine) ([]*SyncResult, error) {
    results := []*SyncResult{}
    
    // 1. Connect to remote
    client, err := s.pool.Get(m)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer s.pool.Release(m.ID)
    
    // 2. Get local profiles
    localProfiles := s.vault.ListAllProfiles()
    
    // 3. Get remote profiles
    remoteProfiles, err := s.listRemoteProfiles(client)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("list remote profiles: %w", err)
    }
    
    // 4. Merge profile lists (union)
    allProfiles := mergeProfileLists(localProfiles, remoteProfiles)
    
    // 5. For each profile, compare and sync
    for _, p := range allProfiles {
        op := s.determineSyncOperation(client, p)
        if op == nil {
            continue // Already in sync
        }
        
        result := s.executeOperation(client, op)
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}
```

### Determine Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) determineSyncOperation(client *SSHClient, p ProfileRef) *SyncOperation {
    localFresh := s.getLocalFreshness(p)
    remoteFresh := s.getRemoteFreshness(client, p)
    
    switch {
    case localFresh == nil && remoteFresh != nil:
        // Only exists on remote: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    case localFresh != nil && remoteFresh == nil:
        // Only exists locally: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(localFresh, remoteFresh):
        // Local is fresher: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(remoteFresh, localFresh):
        // Remote is fresher: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    default:
        // Equal freshness: no action
        return nil
    }
}
```

### Execute Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) executeOperation(client *SSHClient, op *SyncOperation) *SyncResult {
    start := time.Now()
    
    switch op.Direction {
    case "push":
        // Read local files
        files, err := s.vault.ReadProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to remote
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        err = client.BatchWrite(remotePathFiles(remotePath, files))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
    case "pull":
        // Read remote files
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        files, err := client.BatchRead(remoteFilePaths(remotePath))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to local vault
        err = s.vault.WriteProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile, files)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
    }
    
    return &SyncResult{
        Success:  true,
        Duration: time.Since(start),
        ...
    }
}
```

## Conflict Resolution

### Simultaneous Updates
If two machines update the same profile at nearly the same time:
- Both will detect their local as "fresher"
- Both will push
- Result: last write wins (deterministic by timestamp)

This is acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: conflict detection and resolution UI.

### Expired Tokens
Even expired tokens might be useful (for refresh_token). Don't skip expired tokens in sync.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Claude tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Codex tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Gemini tokens
- [ ] Freshness comparison logic correct
- [ ] Push operation works
- [ ] Pull operation works
- [ ] Union merge of profile lists works
- [ ] Full sync algorithm correct
- [ ] Conflict handling deterministic
- [ ] Unit tests for freshness extraction
- [ ] Unit tests for comparison logic
- [ ] Integration test for full sync

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/freshness.go`
- `internal/sync/freshness_test.go`
- `internal/sync/algorithm.go`
- `internal/sync/algorithm_test.go`

-{"id":"caam-ogpo","title":"Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths","description":"# Task: Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths

## Overview
Benchmark tests to ensure critical operations meet the sub-100ms latency target that makes caam feel instant.

## Why This Matters
caam differentiates by being FAST:
- Profile switching: <50ms (not 30 seconds with browser)
- Auth file swap: <10ms
- Rate limit detection: <1ms per line
- PTY injection: <5ms

If these are slow, caam loses its value proposition.

## Benchmarks

### 1. Auth File Swap
```go
func BenchmarkAuthFileSwap(b *testing.B) {
    vault := authfile.NewTestVault(b)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "alice", largeAuthData)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "bob", largeAuthData)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        profile := []string{"alice", "bob"}[i%2]
        vault.SwapActive(profile)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per swap
    // Report: b.ReportMetric(float64(elapsed)/float64(b.N), "ns/swap")
}
```

### 2. Rate Limit Detection
```go
func BenchmarkRateLimitDetection(b *testing.B) {
    detector := ratelimit.NewDetector(ratelimit.DefaultPatterns())
    lines := loadTestLines(1000) // Mix of normal and rate limit lines
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        for _, line := range lines {
            detector.Check(line)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per 1000 lines
}
```

### 3. PTY Command Injection
```go
func BenchmarkPTYInjection(b *testing.B) {
    ctrl := pty.NewTestController(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        ctrl.InjectCommand("/login")
    }
    
    // Target: <5ms per injection
}
```

### 4. JWT Parsing
```go
func BenchmarkJWTParsing(b *testing.B) {
    token := loadTestJWT() // Large JWT with many claims
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        identity.ExtractFromJWT(token)
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per parse
}
```

### 5. Log Parsing (per file)
```go
func BenchmarkLogParsing(b *testing.B) {
    logFile := createTestLogFile(10000) // 10k entries
    scanner := logs.NewClaudeScanner()
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        scanner.ScanFile(ctx, logFile, time.Time{})
    }
    
    // Target: <100ms per 10k entries
}
```

### 6. Burn Rate Calculation
```go
func BenchmarkBurnRateCalc(b *testing.B) {
    entries := generateTestEntries(1000)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        usage.CalculateBurnRate(entries, 2*time.Hour)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per calculation
}
```

### 7. Full Handoff Latency (E2E)
```go
func BenchmarkHandoffLatency(b *testing.B) {
    runner := setupTestRunner(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        start := time.Now()
        runner.SimulateRateLimitAndHandoff()
        elapsed := time.Since(start)
        
        if elapsed > 5*time.Second {
            b.Errorf("Handoff too slow: %v", elapsed)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <5 seconds total (including login wait)
}
```

## Performance Targets Summary
| Operation | Target | Critical |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Auth file swap | <10ms | Yes |
| Rate limit check (per line) | <0.1ms | Yes |
| PTY injection | <5ms | Yes |
| JWT parsing | <1ms | No |
| Log file parse (10k lines) | <100ms | No |
| Burn rate calculation | <10ms | No |
| Full handoff (E2E) | <5s | Yes |

## Reporting
- Run benchmarks in CI
- Track regression over time
- Alert if critical paths exceed targets
- Generate flamegraphs for slow operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All critical paths benchmarked
- [ ] Tests pass on CI
- [ ] Results logged with clear metrics
- [ ] No critical path exceeds target
- [ ] Flamegraph generation for profiling

## Files to Create
- internal/authfile/vault_bench_test.go
- internal/ratelimit/detector_bench_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_bench_test.go
- internal/identity/jwt_bench_test.go
- internal/logs/scanner_bench_test.go
- internal/usage/burnrate_bench_test.go
- internal/exec/handoff_bench_test.go

 {"id":"caam-ojjb","title":"Unit tests for internal/pty controller (cross-platform)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/pty Controller (Cross-Platform)

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the PTY controller including command injection, output monitoring, and cross-platform behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. PTY Creation Tests
```go
func TestPTYCreation(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Creating PTY for simple command")
    
    cmd := exec.Command("cat")
    ctrl, err := pty.NewController(cmd)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Creation result: err=%v", err)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Verifying PTY is functional")
    // Write test input
    ctrl.InjectCommand("hello")
    
    // Read output
    output, err := ctrl.ReadWithTimeout(time.Second)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Read output: %q, err=%v", output, err)
    
    require.Contains(t, output, "hello")
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Closing PTY")
    ctrl.Close()
}
```

### 2. Command Injection Tests
- Inject single command
- Inject command with special characters
- Inject multiple commands rapidly
- Inject while output is streaming

### 3. Pattern Waiting Tests
```go
func TestWaitForPattern(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        pattern  string
        output   string
        timeout  time.Duration
        wantErr  bool
    }{
        {
            name:    "pattern found",
            pattern: "SUCCESS",
            output:  "Processing...\nSUCCESS: done",
            timeout: time.Second,
            wantErr: false,
        },
        {
            name:    "pattern not found - timeout",
            pattern: "SUCCESS",
            output:  "ERROR: failed",
            timeout: 100 * time.Millisecond,
            wantErr: true,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Pattern: %q", tc.pattern)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Expected output: %q", tc.output)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Timeout: %v", tc.timeout)
            
            // ... test execution ...
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Result: err=%v, wantErr=%v", err, tc.wantErr)
        })
    }
}
```

### 4. Platform-Specific Tests
```go
func TestPlatformSupport(t *testing.T) {
    t.Logf("[TEST] Running on: %s/%s", runtime.GOOS, runtime.GOARCH)
    
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "linux", "darwin":
        t.Log("[TEST] Unix PTY support expected")
        testUnixPTY(t)
    case "windows":
        t.Log("[TEST] Windows ConPTY support (or graceful error)")
        testWindowsPTY(t)
    default:
        t.Skipf("[TEST] Unsupported platform: %s", runtime.GOOS)
    }
}
```

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- PTY creation with invalid command
- Read from closed PTY
- Write to closed PTY
- Timeout handling

### 6. Resource Cleanup Tests
- Verify PTY is closed on context cancel
- Verify no file descriptor leaks
- Verify child process is terminated

## Logging Requirements
- Log platform/OS at start
- Log PTY file descriptors
- Log all injected commands
- Log all read output
- Log timing of operations

## Cross-Platform Notes
- Linux/macOS: Uses unix.Openpty or github.com/creack/pty
- Windows: Uses ConPTY or gracefully errors with instructions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PTY creation works on Linux and macOS
- [ ] Command injection is reliable
- [ ] Pattern waiting has proper timeout
- [ ] Resource cleanup is verified
- [ ] Windows either works or errors gracefully
- [ ] No flaky tests

## Files to Create
- internal/pty/controller_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_unix_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_windows_test.go
